findings.title: purgeChatConversations count/delete TOCTOU over-reports rows_affected
findings.why_it_matters: If rows are deleted between the COUNT query and the DELETE query by a concurrent operation (e.g., hardDeleteUser running in another invocation), rows_affected will over-report. The audit log will show N deletions when fewer actually occurred. This is cosmetic inaccuracy only — no data is corrupted and the GDPR goal (deletion) is still achieved.
evidence: Line 237-246: const { count: eligibleCount } = await SELECT count WHERE updated_at < cutoff
Line 256-260: DELETE WHERE updated_at < cutoff (separate query - different instant in time)
rowsToDelete = eligibleCount ?? 0 — used as rows_affected but delete may have matched fewer rows

residual_risks: chat_conversations has no is_pinned distinction — all conversations older than 365d are deleted including any user-pinned ones. Documented and accepted for S11 scope.
testing_gaps: No test exercises the TOCTOU scenario (concurrent deletion between count and delete)
